items.label: "subarray", "contiguous", "window"
items.code: → Sliding Window / Prefix Sum
items.note: Shrinkable or fixed-size window over array

items.label: "substring" with condition
items.code: → Sliding Window + HashMap
items.note: Use HashMap<Character, Integer> for char counts

items.label: "sorted array" + search
items.code: → Binary Search
items.note: lo + (hi - lo) / 2 to avoid overflow

items.label: "in-place", "O(1) space"
items.code: → Two Pointers / Swap
items.note: Read/write pointers or opposite-end squeeze

items.label: "palindrome"
items.code: → Two Pointers (center-expand or edges) / DP
items.note: Expand from center for all palindromic substrings

items.label: "anagram", "permutation of"
items.code: → int[26] freq array or HashMap
items.note: Compare frequency arrays for O(1) check

items.label: "duplicate", "contains", "seen before"
items.code: → HashSet / HashMap
items.note: O(1) lookup is the key insight

items.label: "merge intervals", "overlapping"
items.code: → Sort by start + linear scan
items.note: Arrays.sort(intervals, (a,b) -> a[0] - b[0])

items.label: "two sum", "pair with sum"
items.code: → HashMap (unsorted) / Two Pointers (sorted)
items.note: Store complement in map for O(1) lookup

items.label: "rotate array", "reverse"
items.code: → Reverse sub-sections / Cyclic replacement
items.note: Reverse all, reverse first k, reverse rest

items.label: "majority element", "more than n/2"
items.code: → Boyer-Moore Voting / HashMap counting
items.note: Voting: cancel pairs of different elements

items.label: "product except self"
items.code: → Prefix product + Suffix product
items.note: Build left[] and right[] product arrays

items.label: "shortest path", "minimum steps"
items.code: → BFS (unweighted) / Dijkstra (weighted)
items.note: BFS guarantees shortest in unweighted graphs

items.label: "number of islands", "connected components"
items.code: → DFS / BFS / Union-Find
items.note: Flood fill with visited set or in-place marking

items.label: "level order", "layer by layer"
items.code: → BFS with level size tracking
items.note: int size = queue.size(); loop size times per level

items.label: "lowest common ancestor", "path in tree"
items.code: → Recursive DFS with return values
items.note: Most tree problems = DFS returning info upward

items.label: "topological order", "course schedule"
items.code: → Kahn's BFS (indegree) or DFS post-order
items.note: Build indegree map, start from indegree 0

items.label: "cycle detection" in graph
items.code: → DFS with 3-color (white/gray/black) or Union-Find
items.note: Gray node revisited = back edge = cycle

items.label: "binary tree", "BST" + validate
items.code: → Inorder traversal (BST) / DFS with min-max range
items.note: Valid BST: pass (min, max) bounds down recursively

items.label: "serialize / deserialize tree"
items.code: → BFS or preorder DFS with null markers
items.note: Use "null" string sentinel for missing children

items.label: "clone graph", "deep copy"
items.code: → BFS/DFS + HashMap<original, clone>
items.note: Map old nodes to new nodes to handle cycles

items.label: "minimum cost", "maximum profit", "number of ways"
items.code: → DP (or Greedy if locally optimal = globally optimal)
items.note: Check for optimal substructure + overlapping subproblems

items.label: "longest increasing subsequence"
items.code: → DP O(n²) or patience sort O(n log n)
items.note: Binary search on tails array for O(n log n)

items.label: "partition into subsets", "knapsack"
items.code: → DP with boolean or value table
items.note: dp[i][w] = can we achieve weight w using items 0..i?

items.label: "edit distance", "string matching"
items.code: → 2-D DP on two strings
items.note: dp[i][j] = answer for s[0..i-1] and t[0..j-1]

items.label: "word break", "can segment"
items.code: → 1-D DP with HashSet of words
items.note: dp[i] = can s[0..i-1] be segmented?

items.label: "best time to buy/sell stock"
items.code: → State machine DP / Greedy
items.note: Track states: holding, not holding, cooldown

items.label: "decode ways", "count paths"
items.code: → 1-D DP with conditional transitions
items.note: dp[i] depends on dp[i-1] and dp[i-2] with conditions

items.label: "top k", "k most frequent", "kth largest"
items.code: → Heap (PriorityQueue) / Bucket Sort
items.note: Min-heap of size k for top-k; bucket sort if bounded

items.label: "all combinations", "all permutations"
items.code: → Backtracking
items.note: Enumerate with choose/unchoose pattern

items.label: "parentheses", "brackets", "valid nesting"
items.code: → Stack (ArrayDeque)
items.note: Push opening, pop and match on closing

items.label: "next greater", "next smaller", "stock span"
items.code: → Monotonic Stack
items.note: Maintain decreasing/increasing stack of indices

items.label: n ≤ 20 → O(2^n)
items.code: → Backtracking / Bitmask DP
items.note: Small n = brute force is fine

items.label: n ≤ 10⁵ → O(n log n) or O(n)
items.code: → Sort, Heap, Binary Search, HashMap, Sliding Window
items.note: Most common constraint range in interviews

items.label: n ≤ 500 → O(n²) or O(n³)
items.code: → DP (2D), nested loops, matrix operations
items.note: Quadratic is acceptable for n ≤ 500

items.label: n ≤ 10⁹+ → O(log n) or O(1)
items.code: → Binary Search, Math, Bit Manipulation
items.note: Can't even iterate n; need logarithmic or constant

items.label: "design" problems
items.code:
→ HashMap + doubly-linked list (LRU)
→ HashMap + TreeMap (time-based)
→ Multiple data structures composed
items.note: LRU Cache, LFU Cache, Time Map, RandomizedSet

items.label: "linked list" manipulation
items.code: → Two Pointers (slow/fast) + Dummy Head
items.note: Always consider dummy node to simplify head edge cases

items.label: "matrix rotation", "spiral order"
items.code: → Layer-by-layer traversal / Transpose + Reverse
items.note: Rotate 90°: transpose then reverse each row

items.label: "trie", "prefix matching"
items.code: → Trie (prefix tree) with TrieNode[26] children
items.note: Insert/search O(word length); autocomplete, word search

items.label: "LRU Cache"
items.code: → HashMap + Doubly Linked List (or LinkedHashMap)
items.note: O(1) get and put; move accessed items to front

items.label: "random" + O(1) insert/delete/getRandom
items.code: → ArrayList + HashMap<value, index>
items.note: Swap with last element for O(1) removal

items.label: Two Sum (sorted)
items.note: Works on sorted arrays; adjust pointers based on comparison

items.label: Container With Most Water
items.note: Move the shorter side inward to potentially find taller bar

items.label: Valid Palindrome
items.note: Skip non-alphanumeric with Character.isLetterOrDigit()

items.label: 3Sum
items.note: Sort + fix one element + two-pointer on remainder; skip duplicates

items.label: Trapping Rain Water
items.note: Process from the shorter side; water level bounded by shorter max

items.label: Reverse Linked List (two-pointer)
items.note: Three pointers: prev, curr, next; prev becomes new head

items.label: Remove Duplicates (sorted)
items.note: slow = write pointer, fast = read pointer

items.label: Linked List Cycle
items.note: Floyd's cycle detection; to find start, reset one to head

items.label: Move Zeroes
items.note: Swap non-zero to insertPos; maintains relative order

items.label: Middle of Linked List
items.note: When fast reaches end, slow is at middle

items.label: Partition (Dutch National Flag)
items.note: 3-way partition; used in Sort Colors (LC 75)

items.label: Longest Substring Without Repeating
items.note: HashMap stores last index of each char; jump left past duplicate

items.label: Minimum Window Substring
items.note: Expand right to satisfy, shrink left to minimize; track 'formed' count

items.label: Variable Window Template
items.note: Generic template; left only moves right so total work is O(n)

items.label: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
items.note: Window valid if (window size - max freq char) <= k replacements

items.label: Max Sum of K Consecutive
items.note: Add right element, remove leftmost; O(1) per slide

items.label: Find All Anagrams
items.note: Compare int[26] frequency arrays; window size = p.length()

items.label: Permutation in String
items.note: Same as Find All Anagrams but return boolean on first match

items.label: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
items.note: Shrink window while sum meets target; track minimum length

items.label: Classic Binary Search
items.note: lo + (hi - lo) / 2 prevents int overflow vs (lo + hi) / 2

items.label: First Position of Target
items.note: Returns insertion point (leftmost target or where it would be)

items.label: Last Position of Target
items.note: lo - 1 gives the rightmost index of target

items.label: Binary Search on Answer
items.note: Search the answer space; feasible() checks if mid works

items.label: Koko Eating Bananas (example)
items.note: (p + mid - 1) / mid is ceiling division without floating point

items.label: Search in Rotated Array
items.note: One half is always sorted; check if target falls in the sorted half

items.label: Find Peak Element
items.note: Move toward the higher neighbor; guaranteed to find a peak

items.label: Find Min in Rotated Sorted
items.note: Compare mid with hi; minimum is at the rotation point

items.label: Shortest Path (unweighted)
items.note: visited.add() returns false if already present — clean idiom

items.label: Grid BFS
items.note: Mark visited BEFORE enqueueing to avoid duplicates

items.label: Level-Order Traversal
items.note: Capture queue.size() before inner loop to process one level at a time

items.label: Zigzag Level-Order
items.note: Reverse odd-indexed levels; or use LinkedList.addFirst()

items.label: Topological Sort (Kahn's)
items.note: If order.size() < V, there's a cycle (no valid ordering)

items.label: Multi-source BFS (Rotten Oranges)
items.note: Start BFS from ALL sources simultaneously; track levels for distance

items.label: Word Ladder (BFS state)
items.note: M = word length, N = dict size; try all 26 chars at each position

items.label: Tree DFS (return value)
items.note: Bottom-up: children return info, parent combines

items.label: Graph DFS (visited set)
items.note: Use getOrDefault to handle nodes with no outgoing edges

items.label: Iterative DFS with ArrayDeque
items.note: ArrayDeque is faster than Stack (not synchronized)

items.label: Grid DFS (Number of Islands)
items.note: Modify grid in-place to mark visited; avoids extra boolean[][] array

items.label: Iterative Inorder (BST)
items.note: Produces sorted order for BST; go left as far as possible, then right

items.label: Union-Find (Disjoint Set)
items.note: Path compression + union by rank; nearly O(1) amortized

items.label: DFS with 3-color cycle detection
items.note: White=unvisited, Gray=in-progress, Black=done; gray→gray = cycle

items.label: Tree Diameter
items.note: Diameter passes through some node; track global max of left+right

items.label: Lowest Common Ancestor
items.note: If both children return non-null, current node is LCA

items.label: Validate BST
items.note: Use long for bounds to handle Integer.MIN_VALUE/MAX_VALUE edge cases

items.label: Subsets
items.note: new ArrayList<>(cur) takes a snapshot; remove last = undo choice

items.label: Combination Sum (reuse allowed)
items.note: Pass i (not i+1) to allow reuse; pass i+1 if no reuse

items.label: Permutations (no duplicates)
items.note: boolean[] used tracks which elements are in current permutation

items.label: Permutations (with duplicates)
items.note: Sort + skip same value when previous sibling unused

items.label: N-Queens (constraint placement)
items.note: Track columns and both diagonals with sets; row-col and row+col identify diagonals

items.label: Letter Combinations of Phone
items.note: StringBuilder is more efficient than String for backtracking

items.label: Word Search (grid backtracking)
items.note: Mark cell as visited by replacing char; restore on backtrack

items.label: Generate Parentheses
items.note: Constraints: open < n to add '('; close < open to add ')'

items.label: Climbing Stairs / Fibonacci
items.note: Can optimize to O(1) space with two variables

items.label: House Robber
items.note: dp[i] = max(skip, rob) = max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2] + nums[i])

items.label: Maximum Subarray (Kadane's)
items.note: At each position: start new subarray or extend existing one

items.label: Decode Ways
items.note: Single digit (1-9) and two-digit (10-26) transitions

items.label: Longest Common Subsequence
items.note: Match → diagonal + 1; no match → max(up, left)

items.label: 0/1 Knapsack
items.note: Iterate w backwards to avoid using same item twice; forwards = unbounded

items.label: Unique Paths (grid)
items.note: Only move right or down; each cell = sum of top + left

items.label: Min Path Sum (grid)
items.note: Modify grid in-place to save space; or use separate dp array

items.label: Coin Change (min coins)
items.note: Fill with amount+1 as infinity; forward loop = unlimited coins

items.label: Longest Increasing Subsequence
items.note: Patience sorting; tails[i] = smallest tail of all increasing subsequences of length i+1

items.label: Word Break
items.note: dp[i] = true if s[0..i-1] can be segmented; k = avg word length

items.label: Edit Distance
items.note: Three operations: replace (diagonal), delete (up), insert (left)

items.label: Next Greater Element
items.note: Decreasing stack; when a bigger element comes, it answers all smaller tops

items.label: Daily Temperatures
items.note: Store indices; distance = i - j

items.label: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
items.note: Increasing stack; append 0 sentinel to flush remaining bars

items.label: Monotonic Stack Template
items.note: Change compare() for next greater vs next smaller; store indices not values

items.label: Circular Next Greater
items.note: Iterate twice to handle circular array; push indices only in first pass

items.label: Build Prefix Sum
items.note: prefix[0] = 0 avoids edge cases; sum of [i,j] = prefix[j+1] - prefix[i]

items.label: Range Sum Query
items.note: Precompute once, query any range in constant time

items.label: Subarray Sum = K
items.note: If prefix[j] - prefix[i] = k, then prefix[i] = prefix[j] - k

items.label: Continuous Subarray Sum (mod k)
items.note: If prefix[j] % k == prefix[i] % k, then sum(i+1..j) is divisible by k

items.label: 2-D Prefix Sum
items.note: Inclusion-exclusion; extra row/col of zeros avoids edge cases

items.label: Product Except Self
items.note: Left prefix product in first pass, multiply by right product in second pass

items.label: Merge Overlapping
items.note: Sort by start; extend last interval if overlapping

items.label: Insert & Merge
items.note: Three phases: before overlap, merge overlap, after overlap

items.label: Min Meeting Rooms (sweep line)
items.note: Sort starts and ends separately; compare like merge step

items.label: Non-overlapping Intervals (greedy)
items.note: Greedy: sort by end time, keep intervals that don't overlap; count removals

items.label: Interval overlap check
items.note: Two intervals overlap if neither ends before the other starts

items.label: Interval List Intersection
items.note: Two pointers; advance the interval that ends earlier

items.label: Create
items.note: List.of() returns unmodifiable list (Java 9+)

items.label: add(e) / add(i, e)
items.code:
list.add(42);        // append to end
list.add(0, 99);     // insert at index 0
items.note: End add is O(1); index add shifts elements right

items.label: get(i) / set(i, e)
items.code:
int val = list.get(0);
list.set(0, 100);
items.note: Random access by index

items.label: remove(i) / remove(Object)
items.code:
list.remove(0);                // by index
list.remove(Integer.valueOf(42)); // by value
items.note: remove(int) removes by index; remove(Object) by value — beware ambiguity

items.label: contains / indexOf
items.code:
boolean has = list.contains(42);
int idx = list.indexOf(42); // -1 if not found
items.note: Linear scan; use HashSet for O(1) contains

items.label: size / isEmpty
items.code:
int n = list.size();
boolean empty = list.isEmpty();

items.label: iterate
items.code:
for (int x : list) { /* ... */ }
for (int i = 0; i < list.size(); i++) { int x = list.get(i); }
items.note: Enhanced for-each or indexed loop

items.label: subList
items.code: List<Integer> sub = list.subList(1, 4); // [1, 4) view
items.note: Returns a view, not a copy; changes reflect in original

items.label: sort
items.note: In-place sort; uses TimSort

items.label: toArray
items.code:
Integer[] arr = list.toArray(new Integer[0]);
int[] prim = list.stream().mapToInt(Integer::intValue).toArray();
items.note: No direct toArray for primitives; use stream

items.label: Collections.swap
items.code: Collections.swap(list, i, j);
items.note: Swap two elements by index

items.label: Collections.reverse
items.code: Collections.reverse(list);
items.note: In-place reversal

items.label: Collections.frequency
items.code: int count = Collections.frequency(list, target);
items.note: Count occurrences of an element

items.label: removeIf
items.code: list.removeIf(x -> x < 0);
items.note: Remove elements matching predicate; avoids ConcurrentModificationException

items.label: addAll / containsAll
items.note: containsAll is O(n*m) for ArrayList; use HashSet for O(n+m)

items.label: Collections.min / max
items.note: Works on any Collection; second form takes Comparator

items.label: Collections.nCopies
items.code: List<Integer> zeros = new ArrayList<>(Collections.nCopies(10, 0));
items.note: Create list of n identical elements; useful for initialization

items.label: List.copyOf (immutable)
items.code: List<Integer> immutable = List.copyOf(mutableList); // Java 10+
items.note: Defensive copy; returns unmodifiable list; throws on null elements

items.label: Binary search on list
items.note: List must be sorted; same return convention as Arrays.binarySearch

items.label: filter
items.note: Returns new list; original unchanged

items.label: map
items.note: Transform each element

items.label: collect / toList
items.code:
List<Integer> result = list.stream()
  .filter(x -> x > 0)
  .toList(); // Java 16+ immutable
items.note: .toList() is shorter but returns unmodifiable list

items.label: forEach
items.code:
list.forEach(x -> System.out.println(x));
list.forEach(System.out::println); // method ref
items.note: Side-effect operation; prefer for-each loop in interviews

items.label: reduce to find max
items.code: int max = list.stream().reduce(Integer.MIN_VALUE, Integer::max);
items.note: reduce with identity; or use .max(Comparator.naturalOrder())

items.label: groupBy with streams
items.code:
Map<Integer, List<String>> byLength = list.stream()
  .collect(Collectors.groupingBy(String::length));
items.note: Group elements by a classifier function

items.label: Create
items.code:
Map<String, Integer> map = new HashMap<>();
Map<String, Integer> ordered = new LinkedHashMap<>(); // insertion order
items.note: LinkedHashMap maintains insertion order; TreeMap sorts by key

items.label: put / get
items.code:
map.put("a", 1);
int val = map.get("a"); // null if missing (NPE if unboxed!)
items.note: get returns null for missing key — beware auto-unboxing NPE

items.label: containsKey / containsValue
items.code:
boolean hasKey = map.containsKey("a");
boolean hasVal = map.containsValue(1);
items.note: containsValue is linear scan

items.label: remove
items.code:
map.remove("a");
map.remove("a", 1); // remove only if value matches

items.label: keySet / values / entrySet
items.note: These are views backed by the map

items.label: iterate entries
items.note: var (Java 10+) for concise local type inference

items.label: size / isEmpty
items.code:
int n = map.size();
boolean empty = map.isEmpty();

items.label: Build frequency map
items.note: merge is the cleanest way to count frequencies

items.label: Two Sum pattern
items.note: Store value → index; check complement before inserting

items.label: Default capacity hint
items.code:
Map<String, Integer> map = new HashMap<>(n * 4 / 3 + 1);
// Avoids rehashing when you know approximate size
items.note: Load factor = 0.75; pre-size to avoid resize overhead

items.label: getOrDefault
items.code: int val = map.getOrDefault("key", 0);
items.note: Avoids null checks; great for counting

items.label: putIfAbsent
items.code: map.putIfAbsent("key", 0);
items.note: Only inserts if key not present

items.label: merge
items.code:
map.merge("key", 1, Integer::sum); // increment
map.merge("key", val, Math::max);  // keep max
items.note: If key exists, apply function to old and new value

items.label: compute
items.code: map.compute("key", (k, v) -> v == null ? 1 : v + 1);
items.note: Full control; return null to remove entry

items.label: computeIfAbsent
items.code: map.computeIfAbsent("key", k -> new ArrayList<>()).add(val);
items.note: Create value lazily; perfect for adjacency lists

items.label: replaceAll
items.code: map.replaceAll((k, v) -> v * 2);
items.note: Apply function to every value in the map

items.label: forEach
items.code: map.forEach((k, v) -> System.out.println(k + "=" + v));
items.note: BiConsumer version of iteration

items.label: Adjacency list (graph)
items.note: computeIfAbsent + add is the idiomatic pattern

items.label: Map.of (immutable)
items.code:
Map<String, Integer> map = Map.of("a", 1, "b", 2); // Java 9+
Map<String, Integer> map = Map.ofEntries(
  Map.entry("a", 1), Map.entry("b", 2)
); // 10+ entries
items.note: Unmodifiable; throws on put/remove; max 10 pairs for Map.of()

items.label: TreeMap basics
items.note: Sorted map backed by red-black tree; supports range queries

items.label: Group anagrams pattern
items.note: Sorted string as key groups all anagrams together

items.label: Convert map to sorted entries
items.code:
List<Map.Entry<String, Integer>> sorted = new ArrayList<>(map.entrySet());
sorted.sort(Map.Entry.comparingByValue(Comparator.reverseOrder()));
items.note: Sort entries by value descending; useful for top-k frequency problems

items.label: LRU Cache with LinkedHashMap
items.note: accessOrder=true + removeEldestEntry gives LRU behavior for free

items.label: Bi-directional mapping
items.note: Use two maps for 1-to-1 mapping (e.g., Isomorphic Strings)

items.label: Create
items.note: Set.of() throws on duplicates and is unmodifiable

items.label: add / remove / contains
items.note: add returns boolean — useful as visited.add(x) check

items.label: size / isEmpty / clear
Set operations
items.code:
setA.addAll(setB);     // union (modifies setA)
setA.retainAll(setB);  // intersection
setA.removeAll(setB);  // difference
items.note: These modify setA in-place; copy first if needed

items.label: iterate
items.code: for (int x : set) { /* no guaranteed order */ }
items.note: HashSet: no order; LinkedHashSet: insertion order

items.label: Convert list ↔ set
items.code:
Set<Integer> set = new HashSet<>(list);
List<Integer> list = new ArrayList<>(set);
items.note: Remove duplicates: new ArrayList<>(new HashSet<>(list))

items.label: LinkedHashSet
items.note: Maintains insertion order; useful when order matters + O(1) lookup

items.label: Visited pattern
items.note: add() returns boolean — cleaner than contains() + add() separately

items.label: Longest Consecutive Sequence
items.note: Only start counting from sequence beginnings (no predecessor)

items.label: Set.copyOf (immutable)
items.code: Set<Integer> immutable = Set.copyOf(mutableSet); // Java 10+
items.note: Defensive copy; returns unmodifiable set; throws on null

items.label: Create
items.code:
TreeSet<Integer> ts = new TreeSet<>();
TreeSet<Integer> desc = new TreeSet<>(Comparator.reverseOrder());
items.note: Sorted set backed by red-black tree

items.label: floor / ceiling
items.code:
Integer f = ts.floor(5);   // greatest ≤ 5 (or null)
Integer c = ts.ceiling(5); // smallest ≥ 5 (or null)
items.note: floor/ceiling return null if no match — check for null

items.label: lower / higher
items.code:
Integer lo = ts.lower(5);  // greatest < 5 (strict)
Integer hi = ts.higher(5); // smallest > 5 (strict)
items.note: Strict versions of floor/ceiling

items.label: first / last
items.code:
int min = ts.first(); // smallest
int max = ts.last();  // largest
items.note: Throws NoSuchElementException if empty

items.label: headSet / tailSet / subSet
items.code:
SortedSet<Integer> below = ts.headSet(5);       // < 5
SortedSet<Integer> above = ts.tailSet(5);       // >= 5
SortedSet<Integer> range = ts.subSet(2, 8);     // [2, 8)
items.note: Returns views; headSet exclusive, tailSet inclusive by default

items.label: pollFirst / pollLast
items.code:
Integer min = ts.pollFirst(); // remove & return smallest
Integer max = ts.pollLast();  // remove & return largest
items.note: Returns null if empty; useful as sorted priority structure

items.label: TreeSet for sorted sliding window
items.note: Custom comparator to handle duplicate values using index as tiebreaker

items.label: add / remove / contains
items.code:
ts.add(5);            // O(log n)
ts.remove(5);         // O(log n)
boolean has = ts.contains(5); // O(log n)
items.note: All ops are O(log n) unlike HashSet's O(1) — use when you need sorting

items.label: Create
items.code: Deque<Integer> stack = new ArrayDeque<>();
items.note: Always use ArrayDeque for stack; never use java.util.Stack

items.label: push / pop / peek
items.code:
stack.push(42);          // push to top
int top = stack.pop();   // remove & return top
int look = stack.peek(); // return top without removing
items.note: pop/peek throw NoSuchElementException if empty

items.label: isEmpty / size
items.code:
boolean empty = stack.isEmpty();
int n = stack.size();

items.label: Iterate (top to bottom)
items.code: for (int x : stack) { /* top to bottom */ }
items.note: Iterator goes from top (first pushed last) to bottom

items.label: Stack is legacy
items.code:
// java.util.Stack extends Vector — synchronized, slow
// ArrayDeque is faster (no synchronization overhead)
items.note: Stack is legacy; ArrayDeque is the modern replacement

items.label: Consistent interface
items.note: ArrayDeque works as both stack (LIFO) and queue (FIFO)

items.label: Min Stack pattern
items.note: Store running minimum alongside each element

items.label: Valid Parentheses
items.note: Classic stack problem; check empty stack at end

items.label: Evaluate RPN
items.note: Pop two operands for each operator; push result back

items.label: Decode String
items.note: Two stacks: one for strings, one for repeat counts; push on '[', pop on ']'

items.label: Create min-heap
items.code: PriorityQueue<Integer> minHeap = new PriorityQueue<>();
items.note: Natural ordering = min-heap (smallest first)

items.label: offer / poll / peek
items.code:
minHeap.offer(42);        // insert
int min = minHeap.poll(); // remove & return smallest
int look = minHeap.peek(); // smallest without removing
items.note: poll/peek return null if empty (don't unbox blindly)

items.label: Create max-heap
items.code: PriorityQueue<Integer> maxHeap = new PriorityQueue<>(Collections.reverseOrder());
items.note: reverseOrder() flips to max-heap

items.label: Lambda max-heap
items.code: PriorityQueue<Integer> maxHeap = new PriorityQueue<>((a, b) -> b - a);
items.note: b - a for descending; beware int overflow for extreme values

items.label: Sort by second element
items.code: PriorityQueue<int[]> pq = new PriorityQueue<>((a, b) -> a[1] - b[1]);
items.note: Min by index 1; useful for (value, index) or (cost, node)

items.label: Multi-field comparator
items.note: Comparator.comparingInt avoids overflow from subtraction

items.label: size / isEmpty / contains
items.note: contains is O(n) — heap doesn't support fast lookup

items.label: Top K pattern
items.note: Min-heap of size k; smallest of top-k is the kth largest

items.label: Merge K Sorted Lists
items.note: k = number of lists; dummy head avoids edge cases

items.label: Dijkstra's Algorithm
items.note: Lazy deletion: skip stale entries instead of decrease-key

items.label: remove
items.code: pq.remove(element);
items.note: Linear scan to find element; rarely used in interviews

items.label: Kth Largest in Stream
items.note: Maintain min-heap of size k; peek() is always the kth largest

items.label: Median Finder (two heaps)
items.note: lo (max-heap) holds smaller half; hi (min-heap) holds larger half

items.label: Create
items.code: Queue<Integer> queue = new LinkedList<>();
items.note: LinkedList implements Queue; ArrayDeque is faster for most uses

items.label: offer / poll / peek
items.code:
queue.offer(42);        // enqueue (returns true)
int front = queue.poll(); // dequeue (returns null if empty)
int look = queue.peek();  // front without removing
items.note: offer/poll/peek return special values; add/remove/element throw exceptions

items.label: add vs offer
items.code:
queue.add(42);    // throws IllegalStateException if full
queue.offer(42);  // returns false if full
items.note: Prefer offer/poll/peek in interview code — no exceptions

items.label: size / isEmpty
items.code:
int n = queue.size();
boolean empty = queue.isEmpty();

items.label: Queue from two stacks
items.note: Classic interview problem; each element moves at most twice

items.label: BFS usage (Queue)
items.note: Queue is the backbone of BFS; LinkedList or ArrayDeque

items.label: ArrayDeque vs LinkedList
items.note: ArrayDeque is faster for both stack and queue operations

items.label: Create
items.code: Deque<Integer> deque = new ArrayDeque<>();
items.note: Double-ended queue; faster than LinkedList

items.label: offerFirst / offerLast
items.code:
deque.offerFirst(1); // add to front
deque.offerLast(2);  // add to back

items.label: pollFirst / pollLast
items.code:
int front = deque.pollFirst(); // remove from front
int back = deque.pollLast();   // remove from back
items.note: Returns null if empty

items.label: peekFirst / peekLast
items.code:
int front = deque.peekFirst();
int back = deque.peekLast();

items.label: Sliding Window Maximum
items.note: Monotonic deque: front = max of window; maintain decreasing order

items.label: size / isEmpty
items.code:
int n = deque.size();
boolean empty = deque.isEmpty();

items.label: charAt / length
items.code:
char c = s.charAt(0);
int len = s.length();
items.note: Strings are immutable; length() is a method (not field like array.length)

items.label: substring
items.code: String sub = s.substring(1, 4); // [1, 4)
items.note: Returns new String; end index is exclusive

items.label: indexOf / contains
items.code:
int idx = s.indexOf("ab");    // -1 if not found
boolean has = s.contains("ab");
items.note: contains uses indexOf internally

items.label: split
items.code:
String[] parts = s.split(",");
String[] limited = s.split(",", 3); // max 3 parts
items.note: Takes regex; escape dots as \\.split()

items.label: join
items.code:
String joined = String.join(", ", list);
String joined = String.join("-", "a", "b", "c"); // "a-b-c"
items.note: Static method; works with Iterable or varargs

items.label: replace
items.code:
String r = s.replace('a', 'b');     // all occurrences of char
String r = s.replace("old", "new"); // all occurrences of string
items.note: Returns new String; replaceAll takes regex

items.label: toCharArray
items.code: char[] arr = s.toCharArray();
items.note: Creates a mutable copy; useful for in-place manipulation

items.label: compareTo / equals
items.code:
int cmp = s1.compareTo(s2); // lexicographic: <0, 0, >0
boolean eq = s1.equals(s2); // content equality
items.note: NEVER use == for String comparison; always use .equals()

items.label: trim / strip
items.code:
String t = s.trim();   // remove leading/trailing whitespace
String t = s.strip();  // Unicode-aware trim (Java 11+)
items.note: strip() handles more whitespace chars than trim()

items.label: repeat
items.code: String r = "ab".repeat(3); // "ababab" (Java 11+)
items.note: Useful for building padding strings

items.label: valueOf / parseInt
items.code:
String s = String.valueOf(42);  // int → String
int n = Integer.parseInt("42"); // String → int
items.note: parseInt throws NumberFormatException on invalid input

items.label: toUpperCase / toLowerCase
items.code:
String up = s.toUpperCase();
String lo = s.toLowerCase();
items.note: Returns new String; locale-sensitive

items.label: startsWith / endsWith
items.code:
boolean pre = s.startsWith("abc");
boolean suf = s.endsWith("xyz");
items.note: m = prefix/suffix length

items.label: chars (IntStream)
items.code:
s.chars().filter(c -> c == 'a').count();
s.chars().mapToObj(c -> (char)c).collect(Collectors.toList());
items.note: Returns IntStream of char values; cast to char as needed

items.label: Character utility
items.note: Essential for string manipulation in interviews

items.label: String.format
items.code: String s = String.format("%d:%02d", hours, mins); // "3:05"
items.note: Useful for formatting output; %02d = zero-padded 2 digits

items.label: isEmpty / isBlank
items.code:
boolean empty = s.isEmpty();  // length == 0
boolean blank = s.isBlank();  // only whitespace (Java 11+)
items.note: isBlank returns true for empty or whitespace-only strings

items.label: matches (regex)
items.code:
boolean isNum = s.matches("\\d+");      // all digits
boolean isAlpha = s.matches("[a-zA-Z]+"); // all letters
items.note: Full regex match (like ^...$); use for simple validation

items.label: Create
items.code:
StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("init");
items.note: Mutable; use for string concatenation in loops

items.label: append
items.code: sb.append("hello").append(' ').append(42);
items.note: Chainable; accepts any type (auto-converts to String)

items.label: insert
items.code: sb.insert(0, "prefix");
items.note: Shifts characters right; use sparingly

items.label: delete / deleteCharAt
items.code:
sb.delete(1, 3);    // delete [1, 3)
sb.deleteCharAt(0); // delete single char
items.note: Shifts characters left

items.label: reverse
items.code: sb.reverse();
items.note: In-place reversal; common for palindrome building

items.label: toString
items.code: String result = sb.toString();
items.note: Final conversion back to immutable String

items.label: Why StringBuilder
items.note: String concat in loop = O(n²); StringBuilder = O(n)

items.label: charAt / setCharAt / length
items.note: Unlike String, StringBuilder supports setCharAt for mutation

items.label: Build comma-separated string
items.note: StringJoiner handles delimiter, prefix, suffix; avoids trailing comma

items.label: Declare & init
items.code:
int[] arr = new int[10];           // default 0
int[] arr = {1, 2, 3};
int[][] grid = new int[3][4];      // 3 rows, 4 cols
boolean[] seen = new boolean[26];  // default false
items.note: Primitive arrays default to 0/false/0.0; Object arrays to null

items.label: Array length
items.code: int n = arr.length; // field, not method
items.note: arr.length (no parentheses) vs str.length() vs list.size()

items.label: 2D array init
items.note: Rows can have different lengths (jagged arrays)

items.label: sort
items.code:
Arrays.sort(arr);               // ascending
Arrays.sort(arr, 0, k);        // sort subrange [0, k)
Integer[] boxed = {3,1,2};
Arrays.sort(boxed, Comparator.reverseOrder()); // descending
items.note: Primitive sort = dual-pivot quicksort; Object sort = TimSort

items.label: binarySearch
items.code:
int idx = Arrays.binarySearch(arr, target);
// returns index if found, or -(insertion point) - 1
items.note: Array must be sorted; negative result = not found

items.label: fill
items.code:
Arrays.fill(arr, -1);          // fill entire array
Arrays.fill(arr, 0, 5, -1);   // fill [0, 5) with -1
items.note: Common for initializing DP arrays to sentinel values

items.label: copyOf / copyOfRange
items.code:
int[] copy = Arrays.copyOf(arr, arr.length);
int[] sub = Arrays.copyOfRange(arr, 1, 4); // [1, 4)
items.note: Returns a new array; useful for creating snapshots

items.label: equals / deepEquals
items.code:
boolean eq = Arrays.equals(a, b);         // 1D
boolean deq = Arrays.deepEquals(grid1, grid2); // 2D+
items.note: == compares references; .equals() compares contents

items.label: asList
items.note: asList returns fixed-size list backed by array

items.label: stream
items.note: IntStream for int[]; Stream<Integer> for Integer[]

items.label: toString / deepToString
items.code:
System.out.println(Arrays.toString(arr));       // [1, 2, 3]
System.out.println(Arrays.deepToString(grid));  // [[1,2],[3,4]]
items.note: Essential for debugging; arrays don't override toString()

items.label: Custom sort (2D array)
items.note: Lambda comparator; use Comparator.comparingInt to avoid overflow

items.label: Array max / min / sum
items.note: Stream operations; or use manual loop for no-overhead version

items.label: Swap elements
items.code: int tmp = arr[i]; arr[i] = arr[j]; arr[j] = tmp;
items.note: No built-in swap; must use temp variable

items.label: Reverse array
items.note: Two-pointer swap from edges to center

items.label: 2D array row/col iteration
items.note: grid.length = rows; grid[0].length = cols

items.label: Direction arrays (4-dir / 8-dir)
items.note: Standard pattern for grid BFS/DFS; check bounds before accessing

items.label: Clone / deep copy
items.note: clone() for 1D; manual loop for 2D deep copy

items.label: Matrix transpose
items.note: Swap rows and columns; for rotation: transpose then reverse rows

items.label: Spiral order traversal
items.note: Shrink boundaries after each direction; check bounds before bottom and left

items.label: Convert int[] to Integer[]
items.code:
Integer[] boxed = Arrays.stream(arr).boxed().toArray(Integer[]::new);
int[] unboxed = Arrays.stream(boxed).mapToInt(Integer::intValue).toArray();
items.note: Needed for sorting with custom Comparator (can't use lambda on int[])

items.label: From collection
items.code: Stream<String> s = list.stream();
items.note: Most common way to create a stream

items.label: From array
items.code:
IntStream is = Arrays.stream(arr);         // int[]
Stream<String> ss = Arrays.stream(strArr); // String[]
items.note: IntStream for primitives; Stream<T> for objects

items.label: Stream.of / generate / iterate
items.note: generate/iterate are infinite — always use limit()

items.label: IntStream.range / rangeClosed
items.code:
IntStream.range(0, 10)       // 0..9
IntStream.rangeClosed(1, 10) // 1..10
items.note: Useful for index-based operations

items.label: String chars stream
items.code: long vowels = s.chars().filter(c -> "aeiou".indexOf(c) >= 0).count();
items.note: String.chars() returns IntStream of char values

items.label: Parallel stream
items.code: list.parallelStream().filter(x -> x > 0).collect(Collectors.toList());
items.note: Uses ForkJoinPool; avoid for small data or side effects

items.label: filter
items.code: stream.filter(x -> x > 0)
items.note: Keep elements matching predicate

items.label: map
items.code:
stream.map(x -> x * 2)           // Stream<T> → Stream<R>
stream.mapToInt(Integer::intValue) // Stream → IntStream
items.note: mapToInt avoids boxing when you need primitives

items.label: flatMap
items.code:
stream.flatMap(list -> list.stream())
// [[1,2],[3,4]] → [1,2,3,4]
items.note: Flatten nested structures; one-to-many transformation

items.label: sorted
items.code:
stream.sorted()                          // natural order
stream.sorted(Comparator.reverseOrder()) // descending
stream.sorted(Comparator.comparing(Person::getName))
items.note: Returns new sorted stream; original unchanged

items.label: distinct
items.code: stream.distinct() // removes duplicates (uses .equals())
items.note: Like putting into a Set

items.label: limit / skip
items.code:
stream.limit(5)  // take first 5
stream.skip(3)   // skip first 3
items.note: Combine for pagination: skip(offset).limit(pageSize)

items.label: peek (debugging)
items.code: stream.peek(x -> System.out.println(x)).filter(...)
items.note: Inspect elements mid-pipeline without consuming; for debugging only

items.label: mapToInt / mapToLong
items.code:
int sum = stream.mapToInt(String::length).sum();
long total = stream.mapToLong(x -> x).sum();
items.note: Convert to primitive stream for sum/max/min/average

items.label: collect (toList)
items.code:
List<Integer> list = stream.collect(Collectors.toList());
List<Integer> list = stream.toList(); // Java 16+, immutable
items.note: Collectors.toList() is mutable; .toList() is not

items.label: forEach
items.code: stream.forEach(System.out::println);
items.note: Terminal; cannot use stream again after this

items.label: reduce
items.code:
int sum = stream.reduce(0, Integer::sum);
Optional<Integer> max = stream.reduce(Integer::max);
items.note: First form has identity; second returns Optional

items.label: count
items.code: long n = stream.filter(x -> x > 0).count();
items.note: Returns long, not int

items.label: anyMatch / allMatch / noneMatch
items.note: Short-circuit: stops as soon as answer is known

items.label: findFirst
items.code: Optional<Integer> first = stream.filter(x -> x > 0).findFirst();
items.note: Returns Optional; use with .orElse() or .ifPresent()

items.label: toArray
items.code:
int[] arr = intStream.toArray();
String[] arr = stream.toArray(String[]::new);
items.note: IntStream.toArray() returns int[]; Stream needs array constructor

items.label: min / max
items.code:
Optional<Integer> max = stream.max(Comparator.naturalOrder());
int intMax = intStream.max().orElse(0);
items.note: Stream<T> needs Comparator; IntStream returns OptionalInt

items.label: sum / average (IntStream)
items.code:
int sum = intStream.sum();
OptionalDouble avg = intStream.average();
items.note: Only available on IntStream/LongStream/DoubleStream, not Stream<T>

items.label: toList / toSet
items.code:
List<T> list = stream.collect(Collectors.toList());
Set<T> set = stream.collect(Collectors.toSet());
items.note: toList = ArrayList; toSet = HashSet

items.label: toMap
items.code:
Map<String, Integer> map = stream.collect(
  Collectors.toMap(Person::getName, Person::getAge));
items.note: Throws on duplicate keys; add merge function: (a,b) -> a

items.label: groupingBy
items.code:
Map<String, List<Person>> byCity = stream.collect(
  Collectors.groupingBy(Person::getCity));
items.note: Like SQL GROUP BY; returns Map<K, List<V>>

items.label: joining
items.code:
String csv = stream.map(String::valueOf)
  .collect(Collectors.joining(", "));
items.note: Concatenate strings with delimiter

items.label: counting
items.code:
Map<String, Long> freq = stream.collect(
  Collectors.groupingBy(Function.identity(), Collectors.counting()));
items.note: Frequency map with streams

items.label: partitioningBy
items.code:
Map<Boolean, List<Integer>> parts = stream.collect(
  Collectors.partitioningBy(x -> x % 2 == 0));
items.note: Split into two groups: true-key and false-key

items.label: toUnmodifiableList/Set/Map
items.code: List<T> list = stream.collect(Collectors.toUnmodifiableList()); // Java 10+
items.note: Returns immutable collection; throws NPE on null elements

items.label: summarizingInt
items.note: All summary statistics in one pass

items.label: toMap with merge
items.code:
Map<String, Integer> map = stream.collect(
  Collectors.toMap(keyFn, valueFn, (v1, v2) -> v1)); // keep first on conflict
items.note: Third arg = merge function for duplicate keys; without it, duplicates throw

items.label: groupingBy + downstream
items.note: Downstream collector processes each group (counting, maxBy, mapping, etc.)

items.label: of / ofNullable / empty
items.code:
Optional<String> opt = Optional.of("hello");     // must not be null
Optional<String> opt = Optional.ofNullable(val);  // null → empty
Optional<String> opt = Optional.empty();           // no value
items.note: of() throws NPE if null; use ofNullable() when value might be null

items.label: isPresent / isEmpty
items.code:
if (opt.isPresent()) { /* has value */ }
if (opt.isEmpty()) { /* no value */ } // Java 11+
items.note: isEmpty() is the inverse of isPresent()

items.label: ifPresent
items.code: opt.ifPresent(val -> System.out.println(val));
items.note: Execute action only if value present

items.label: orElse / orElseGet
items.code:
String val = opt.orElse("default");
String val = opt.orElseGet(() -> computeDefault());
items.note: orElse always evaluates default; orElseGet is lazy

items.label: orElseThrow
items.code:
String val = opt.orElseThrow();
String val = opt.orElseThrow(() -> new IllegalStateException("missing"));
items.note: No-arg form throws NoSuchElementException (Java 10+)

items.label: map
items.code: Optional<Integer> len = opt.map(String::length);
items.note: Transform value if present; returns Optional

items.label: flatMap
items.code: Optional<String> result = opt.flatMap(this::findById);
items.note: Use when mapper itself returns Optional (avoids Optional<Optional>)

items.label: filter
items.code: Optional<String> filtered = opt.filter(s -> s.length() > 5);
items.note: Keep value only if predicate matches; else empty

items.label: or (Java 9+)
items.code: Optional<String> result = opt.or(() -> Optional.of("fallback"));
items.note: Return alternative Optional if empty; lazy evaluation

items.label: stream (Java 9+)
items.code: Stream<String> s = opt.stream(); // 0 or 1 element stream
items.note: Useful with flatMap to filter empty Optionals from a stream

items.label: ifPresentOrElse (Java 9+)
items.note: Handle both present and empty cases

items.label: for loop
items.code:
for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) { /* ... */ }
for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--) { /* reverse */ }
items.note: Standard indexed loop; i is scoped to the loop

items.label: while
items.code: while (condition) { /* ... */ }
items.note: Check condition before each iteration

items.label: do-while
items.code: do { /* ... */ } while (condition);
items.note: Executes body at least once; rarely used in interviews

items.label: enhanced for-each
items.code:
for (int x : nums) { /* ... */ }
for (Map.Entry<String, Integer> e : map.entrySet()) { /* ... */ }
items.note: Works on arrays and Iterable; cannot modify collection during iteration

items.label: forEach (Collection)
items.code:
list.forEach(x -> System.out.println(x));
map.forEach((k, v) -> System.out.println(k + "=" + v));
items.note: Functional style; cannot break/continue from lambda

items.label: Iterator
items.note: Only way to safely remove during iteration

items.label: ListIterator
items.note: Bidirectional; supports set() and add() during iteration

items.label: Enumerate pattern (index + value)
items.note: Java has no built-in enumerate; use indexed for loop

items.label: Iterate map entries
items.note: var (Java 10+) for concise local types; entrySet is most efficient

items.label: Stream with index
items.note: Workaround for no built-in indexed forEach

items.label: Nested loop with pairs
items.note: n*(n-1)/2 pairs; start j from i+1 to avoid duplicates

items.label: Bit manipulation loops
items.note: Enumerate all 2^n subsets using bitmask; bit i set = include element i

items.label: Two-pointer loop pattern
items.note: Outer loop moves right; inner while moves left as needed

items.label: break with label
items.note: Breaks out of the labeled outer loop; useful for nested loops

items.label: continue with label
items.note: Skip to next iteration of outer loop

items.label: Comparable interface
items.note: Natural ordering; return negative/zero/positive

items.label: Comparator interface
items.note: External ordering; can have multiple comparators

items.label: Arrays.sort
items.code:
Arrays.sort(arr);                     // primitives: ascending
Arrays.sort(boxed, (a, b) -> b - a); // objects: custom order
items.note: Primitives use dual-pivot quicksort; objects use TimSort (stable)

items.label: Collections.sort
items.code:
Collections.sort(list);
Collections.sort(list, (a, b) -> a - b);
items.note: Equivalent to list.sort(comparator)

items.label: Sort stability
items.note: Stable sort preserves relative order of equal elements

items.label: Bucket Sort
items.note: O(n) when value range k is bounded; used in Top K frequency problems

items.label: Lambda comparator
items.code:
list.sort((a, b) -> a.length() - b.length());
Arrays.sort(intervals, (a, b) -> a[0] - b[0]);
items.note: Concise inline comparator; beware int overflow

items.label: Comparator.comparing
items.code:
list.sort(Comparator.comparing(String::length));
list.sort(Comparator.comparing(Person::getAge));
items.note: Type-safe; extracts key then compares naturally

items.label: thenComparing
items.code:
list.sort(Comparator.comparing(Person::getCity)
  .thenComparing(Person::getName));
items.note: Multi-level sort; first by city, then by name

items.label: reversed
items.code: list.sort(Comparator.comparing(String::length).reversed());
items.note: Flip any comparator to descending

items.label: comparingInt (overflow-safe)
items.code: list.sort(Comparator.comparingInt(a -> a[0]));
items.note: Avoids int overflow that (a-b) can cause for extreme values

items.label: naturalOrder / reverseOrder
items.note: nullsFirst/nullsLast handle null elements safely

items.label: Custom record/class sort
items.note: Records (Java 16+) auto-generate constructor, equals, hashCode, toString

items.label: Sort String chars
items.note: Common for anagram grouping: sorted string as map key

items.label: Counting sort (bounded range)
items.note: O(n) when range k is small; basis for radix sort

items.label: Custom sort with map
items.note: Sort by frequency ascending, then by value descending

items.label: == vs .equals() for objects
items.note: == compares references; .equals() compares content. ALWAYS use .equals() for objects

items.label: String pool
items.note: Literals are interned; new String() is not. Still use .equals() always

items.label: Integer cache (-128 to 127)
items.note: Java caches Integer -128 to 127; outside this range == fails. Use .equals()

items.label: Integer ↔ int pitfalls
items.note: Always check for null before unboxing, or use getOrDefault

items.label: Null unboxing NPE
items.note: Unboxing null Integer/Long/Boolean etc. always throws NPE

items.label: Comparing boxed types
items.note: Unbox explicitly or use .equals() for boxed comparisons

items.label: int overflow
items.note: Java int overflows silently; no exception thrown

items.label: Mid-point overflow
items.code:
int mid = (lo + hi) / 2;           // WRONG if lo+hi overflows
int mid = lo + (hi - lo) / 2;     // SAFE
items.note: Always use lo + (hi - lo) / 2 in binary search

items.label: Math.addExact / multiplyExact
items.code:
int safe = Math.addExact(a, b);       // throws ArithmeticException on overflow
long safe = Math.multiplyExact(a, b); // same for multiply
items.note: Use when you want overflow detection rather than silent wraparound

items.label: Common NPE patterns
items.note: Defensive: constant.equals(variable) avoids NPE

items.label: Optional for safety
items.code:
Optional.ofNullable(map.get("key"))
  .orElse(defaultValue);
items.note: Wrap nullable returns in Optional for safe handling

items.label: Type erasure
items.code:
// At runtime, List<Integer> and List<String> are both just List
// Can't do: new T[] or instanceof List<Integer>
items.note: Generic type info is erased at compile time

items.label: Can't use primitives in generics
items.note: Use Integer, Long, Double etc. for collections; convert to primitive arrays via streams

items.label: Diamond operator
items.code:
Map<String, List<Integer>> map = new HashMap<>(); // infers types
// Before Java 7: new HashMap<String, List<Integer>>()
items.note: Diamond <> infers generic types from left side (Java 7+)

items.label: Arrays.asList pitfall
items.note: asList returns fixed-size list; wrap in ArrayList for mutability

items.label: int[] in Collections
items.note: Autoboxing doesn't work for arrays; must box explicitly

items.label: ConcurrentModificationException
items.note: Never modify a collection during enhanced for-each; use Iterator or removeIf

items.label: HashMap key mutability
items.note: Keys must be immutable or at least not mutated after insertion

items.label: char vs Character in generics
items.note: Use Character wrapper for generics; char autoboxes

items.label: String.split edge cases
items.code:
"a,,b".split(",")   // ["a", "", "b"] — 3 elements
"a,,b,".split(",")  // ["a", "", "b"] — trailing empty dropped!
"a,,b,".split(",", -1) // ["a", "", "b", ""] — keep all
items.note: split drops trailing empty strings by default; use -1 limit to keep them

items.label: Integer.MIN_VALUE negation
items.code:
int x = Integer.MIN_VALUE; // -2147483648
int y = -x; // still -2147483648! (overflow)
int z = Math.abs(x); // still -2147483648!
items.note: MIN_VALUE has no positive counterpart in int; use long for safe negation

items.label: Array index out of bounds
items.note: Unlike Python, negative indices are not supported

items.label: Collections.unmodifiableList
items.note: Unmodifiable is a wrapper; changes to original still reflect

items.label: HashMap iteration order
items.note: If you need deterministic order, don't use plain HashMap

items.label: int division truncation
items.code:
int a = 7 / 2;      // 3 (truncates toward zero)
int b = -7 / 2;     // -3 (NOT -4)
double c = 7.0 / 2;  // 3.5 (floating point)
items.note: Integer division truncates toward zero; cast to double for real division

items.label: Modulo with negatives
items.code:
int r = -7 % 3; // -1 in Java (NOT 2 like Python)
// For positive modulo: ((n % m) + m) % m
items.note: Java % preserves sign of dividend; adjust for algorithms needing positive remainder

items.label: PriorityQueue is NOT sorted
items.note: Heap only guarantees top element; must poll() for sorted output

items.label: Stack.push vs Deque.push
items.note: ArrayDeque.push/pop work on front; Iterator goes front to back

items.label: Bit shift precedence
items.code:
int wrong = 1 << n - 1;  // = 1 << (n-1) due to precedence!
int right = (1 << n) - 1; // 2^n - 1 (all n bits set)
items.note: Arithmetic ops have higher precedence than shift; always parenthesize

items.label: StringBuilder vs String +
items.note: Single-line concat is fine; loop concat must use StringBuilder
